div.h1: Per-PID State Hash Map — Structure and Lookup
div.p: pid_map_t · Open-Addressing · 64 slots · Fibonacci Hash · x86_64 Linux

g.text: 0x18 | struct timespec| entry_time // CLOCK_MONOTONIC at entry
pid_map_t [64 slots × 568 bytes = 35.5 KB — fits in L2 cache]
Collision: PID 1001 → hash=41 (taken by PID 1000) → probe to slot 42
L1 cache | 32 KB | 4 cycles | MISSES: table too large
L2 cache | 256 KB | 12 cycles | HIT: full table fits (35.5 KB)
L3 cache | 8 MB | 40 cycles | always hot
DRAM | — | 200 cycles| never needed for map ops
hot slots | ~3×568B | 27 KB — mostly in L1 after warmup
Linear probe: try slot 42 → empty → place PID 1001 at slot 42
PID 1002: hash(1002) = 42 COLLISION → probe slot 43 → place there
WARNING: zeroing slot on remove can break probe chains
Safe at load < 50%: 3 active / 64 slots = 4.7% load factor
